1	// Package try — Attempt is the C2 shell that wraps the existing
2	// agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig + agent.LandBeadResult + (optional)
3	// agent.DefaultBeadReviewer pipeline and returns a try.Outcome via the C1
4	// ToOutcome adapter. It has no production callers at end of C2; the
5	// orchestrator continues to drive the legacy executor directly. Callers
6	// migrate in C3+.
7	package try
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
16	)
17	
18	// AttemptOpts bundles the knobs Attempt needs to drive the legacy
19	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig + LandBeadResult + DefaultBeadReviewer pipeline.
20	//
21	// Tests inject Executor to supply a canned ExecuteBeadReport without
22	// touching git or a real harness. When Executor is non-nil it short-circuits
23	// the legacy pipeline entirely; the remaining fields are ignored.
24	type AttemptOpts struct {
25		// Executor is an optional injection seam. When non-nil, Attempt delegates
26		// to it instead of calling ExecuteBeadWithConfig + LandBeadResult.
27		Executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
28	
29		// ProjectRoot is the repo the bead executes in.
30		ProjectRoot string
31	
32		// Rcfg is the resolved harness/model/profile config for the attempt.
33		Rcfg config.ResolvedConfig
34	
35		// Runtime carries non-durable injection seams (BeadEvents, AgentRunner, …).
36		Runtime agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime
37	
38		// GitOps is the worker-side git surface (worktree add/remove, head, …).
39		GitOps agent.GitOps
40	
41		// OrchestratorGitOps is the parent-side git surface (UpdateRef).
42		OrchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps
43	
44		// LandingOpts controls how LandBeadResult lands a completed worker result.
45		LandingOpts agent.BeadLandingOptions
46	
47		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is invoked after a successful merge to validate
48		// the commit against the bead's AC. When nil, post-merge review is skipped.
49		Reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
50	}
51	
52	// Attempt runs one bead attempt and returns a try.Outcome.
53	//
54	// When opts.Executor is non-nil, Attempt delegates to it and converts the
55	// returned ExecuteBeadReport via ToOutcome — the path covered by
56	// TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor.
57	//
58	// Otherwise Attempt drives the legacy pipeline:
59	//
60	//  1. agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig — run the agent in an isolated worktree.
61	//  2. agent.LandBeadResult — merge or preserve, evaluate gates.
62	//  3. opts.Reviewer.ReviewBead (best effort) — optional post-merge review.
63	//
64	// The store argument is reserved for the C3+ migration where Attempt will
65	// own bead-tracker bookkeeping (claim, heartbeat, close). At C2 it is
66	// accepted but not yet used; the legacy loop still owns those calls.
67	func Attempt(ctx context.Context, store agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID string, opts AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error) {
68		_ = store // reserved for C3+ migration; legacy loop still owns store I/O.
69	
70		if opts.Executor != nil {
71			report, err := opts.Executor.Execute(ctx, beadID)
72			if err != nil {
73				out := ToOutcome(report)
74				if out.BeadID == "" {
75					out.BeadID = beadID
76				}
77				return out, err
78			}
79			out := ToOutcome(report)
80			if out.BeadID == "" {
81				out.BeadID = beadID
82			}
83			return out, nil
84		}
85	
86		res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, opts.ProjectRoot, beadID, opts.Rcfg, opts.Runtime, opts.GitOps)
87		if execErr != nil && res == nil {
88			return Outcome{BeadID: beadID}, execErr
89		}
90	
91		if res != nil {
92			landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(opts.ProjectRoot, res, opts.OrchestratorGitOps, opts.LandingOpts)
93			if landErr == nil {
94				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
95			} else if execErr == nil {
96				execErr = landErr
97			}
98		}
99	
100		if opts.Reviewer != nil && res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
101			// C3 (ddx-a921ff01): drive the full post-merge review state machine
102			// — verdict events, close-on-APPROVE, reopen-on-REQUEST_CHANGES /
103			// BLOCK, triage callout — via the extracted RunPostMergeReview that
104			// replaces the 180-line inline review block in execute_bead_loop.go.
105			// Store errors are swallowed at this layer because the legacy loop
106			// (which currently owns claim/heartbeat/close bookkeeping) handles
107			// them via handleOutcomeStoreError; the C3 shell predates that
108			// migration (C7) and so cannot route the same way.
109			report := agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
110				BeadID:      beadID,
111				AttemptID:   res.AttemptID,
112				Harness:     res.Harness,
113				Provider:    res.Provider,
114				Model:       res.Model,
115				ActualPower: res.ActualPower,
116				SessionID:   res.SessionID,
117				BaseRev:     res.BaseRev,
118				ResultRev:   res.ResultRev,
119			}
120			_ = agent.RunPostMergeReview(ctx, agent.PostMergeReviewInput{
121				Bead:        bead.Bead{ID: beadID},
122				Report:      report,
123				Reviewer:    opts.Reviewer,
124				Store:       store,
125				ProjectRoot: opts.ProjectRoot,
126				Rcfg:        opts.Rcfg,
127				Assignee:    opts.Rcfg.Assignee(),
128				Now:         time.Now,
129			})
130		}
131	
132		report := reportFromResult(res)
133		out := ToOutcome(report)
134		if out.BeadID == "" {
135			out.BeadID = beadID
136		}
137		return out, execErr
138	}
139	
140	// reportFromResult projects the subset of ExecuteBeadResult fields that
141	// ToOutcome consumes. It is intentionally narrow — the loop's full report
142	// synthesis (tier, profile telemetry, escalation count) is not needed for
143	// the C2 shell and gets built out as callers migrate in C3+.
144	func reportFromResult(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
145		if res == nil {
146			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}
147		}
148		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
149			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
150			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
151			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
152			Harness:            res.Harness,
153			Provider:           res.Provider,
154			Model:              res.Model,
155			ActualPower:        res.ActualPower,
156			Status:             res.Status,
157			Detail:             res.Detail,
158			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
159			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
160			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
161			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
162			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
163			CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
164			DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
165		}
166	}

1	// Package try defines the sealed Outcome sum type that the execute-bead
2	// orchestrator will return once the C-series refactor lands. The types in
3	// this file are introduced ahead of any caller (see ddx-240f2082) so that
4	// later beads can migrate the orchestrator to produce Outcome values
5	// without simultaneously inventing the vocabulary.
6	package try
7	
8	import (
9		"fmt"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14	)
15	
16	// Disposition is the closed set of post-attempt routing decisions the
17	// execute-loop can take on a bead. Every Outcome carries exactly one.
18	type Disposition int
19	
20	const (
21		DispositionMerged Disposition = iota
22		DispositionAlreadyDone
23		DispositionRetry
24		DispositionPark
25		DispositionNeedsHuman
26		DispositionLoopError
27	)
28	
29	func (d Disposition) String() string {
30		switch d {
31		case DispositionMerged:
32			return "merged"
33		case DispositionAlreadyDone:
34			return "already_done"
35		case DispositionRetry:
36			return "retry"
37		case DispositionPark:
38			return "park"
39		case DispositionNeedsHuman:
40			return "needs_human"
41		case DispositionLoopError:
42			return "loop_error"
43		default:
44			return fmt.Sprintf("disposition(%d)", int(d))
45		}
46	}
47	
48	// ParkReason enumerates the closed vocabulary of reasons the loop may park
49	// a bead. String-typed for ergonomic comparison, but every value used in
50	// production must come from the constants below; ParkReasonValid enforces
51	// that at the boundary.
52	type ParkReason string
53	
54	const (
55		ParkReasonNeedsReview              ParkReason = "needs_review"
56		ParkReasonDecomposition            ParkReason = "decomposition"
57		ParkReasonPushFailed               ParkReason = "push_failed"
58		ParkReasonPushConflict             ParkReason = "push_conflict"
59		ParkReasonCostCap                  ParkReason = "cost_cap"
60		ParkReasonLoopError                ParkReason = "loop_error"
61		ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified      ParkReason = "no_changes_unverified"
62		ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified     ParkReason = "no_changes_unjustified"
63		ParkReasonRateLimitBudgetExhausted ParkReason = "rate_limit_budget_exhausted"
64		ParkReasonQuotaPaused              ParkReason = "quota_paused"
65		ParkReasonLockContention           ParkReason = "lock_contention"
66	)
67	
68	// AllParkReasons returns the canonical list of ParkReason values in a
69	// stable order. Used by tests and by callers that need to enumerate the
70	// vocabulary (e.g. for documentation generators).
71	func AllParkReasons() []ParkReason {
72		return []ParkReason{
73			ParkReasonNeedsReview,
74			ParkReasonDecomposition,
75			ParkReasonPushFailed,
76			ParkReasonPushConflict,
77			ParkReasonCostCap,
78			ParkReasonLoopError,
79			ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified,
80			ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified,
81			ParkReasonRateLimitBudgetExhausted,
82			ParkReasonQuotaPaused,
83			ParkReasonLockContention,
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// ParkReasonValid reports whether r is a member of the closed ParkReason
88	// vocabulary. The empty string is invalid; use a zero ParkReason only on
89	// non-Park dispositions.
90	func ParkReasonValid(r ParkReason) bool {
91		for _, p := range AllParkReasons() {
92			if p == r {
93				return true
94			}
95		}
96		return false
97	}
98	
99	func (p ParkReason) String() string { return string(p) }
100	
101	// Outcome is the sealed sum type the execute-loop orchestrator will return
102	// for each bead attempt. It carries the routing decision (Disposition)
103	// along with the small set of facts the loop needs to apply that decision:
104	// timing, cost, the resulting commit (if any), and the bead-tracker events
105	// to record.
106	type Outcome struct {
107		BeadID       string
108		AttemptID    string
109		BaseRev      string
110		ResultRev    string
111		SessionID    string
112		DurationMS   int64
113		CostUSD      float64
114		Disposition  Disposition
115		Cooldown     time.Duration
116		ParkReason   ParkReason
117		RecordEvents []bead.BeadEvent
118	}
119	
120	// dispositionToStatus maps a (Disposition, ParkReason) pair onto the
121	// canonical execute-bead status string. The mapping is deterministic and
122	// invertible for the canonical reports produced by FromOutcome.
123	func dispositionToStatus(d Disposition, p ParkReason) string {
124		switch d {
125		case DispositionMerged:
126			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
127		case DispositionAlreadyDone:
128			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
129		case DispositionRetry:
130			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
131		case DispositionNeedsHuman:
132			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
133		case DispositionLoopError:
134			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
135		case DispositionPark:
136			switch p {
137			case ParkReasonNeedsReview:
138				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
139			case ParkReasonDecomposition:
140				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition
141			case ParkReasonPushFailed:
142				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
143			case ParkReasonPushConflict:
144				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
145			case ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified, ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified:
146				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
147			default:
148				// Park reasons without a dedicated status share execution_failed
149				// and rely on Detail (= ParkReason) to disambiguate on inverse.
150				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
151			}
152		}
153		return ""
154	}
155	
156	// FromOutcome produces the canonical ExecuteBeadReport that ToOutcome
157	// inverts. RecordEvents are not carried (ExecuteBeadReport has no events
158	// field); callers that need events handle them out-of-band.
159	func FromOutcome(o Outcome) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
160		rep := agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
161			BeadID:     o.BeadID,
162			AttemptID:  o.AttemptID,
163			Status:     dispositionToStatus(o.Disposition, o.ParkReason),
164			SessionID:  o.SessionID,
165			BaseRev:    o.BaseRev,
166			ResultRev:  o.ResultRev,
167			CostUSD:    o.CostUSD,
168			DurationMS: o.DurationMS,
169		}
170		if o.Cooldown > 0 {
171			rep.RetryAfter = o.Cooldown.String()
172		}
173		if o.Disposition == DispositionPark && o.ParkReason != "" {
174			rep.Detail = string(o.ParkReason)
175		}
176		return rep
177	}
178	
179	// ToOutcome decodes an ExecuteBeadReport back into an Outcome. It is the
180	// inverse of FromOutcome on canonical reports; reports produced by other
181	// code paths may carry fields Outcome does not model, which are dropped.
182	func ToOutcome(r agent.ExecuteBeadReport) Outcome {
183		o := Outcome{
184			BeadID:     r.BeadID,
185			AttemptID:  r.AttemptID,
186			BaseRev:    r.BaseRev,
187			ResultRev:  r.ResultRev,
188			SessionID:  r.SessionID,
189			DurationMS: r.DurationMS,
190			CostUSD:    r.CostUSD,
191		}
192		if r.RetryAfter != "" {
193			if d, err := time.ParseDuration(r.RetryAfter); err == nil {
194				o.Cooldown = d
195			}
196		}
197		switch r.Status {
198		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
199			o.Disposition = DispositionMerged
200		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
201			o.Disposition = DispositionAlreadyDone
202		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
203			// Detail carrying a valid ParkReason promotes to Park; otherwise Retry.
204			if pr := ParkReason(r.Detail); ParkReasonValid(pr) &&
205				(pr == ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified || pr == ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified) {
206				o.Disposition = DispositionPark
207				o.ParkReason = pr
208			} else {
209				o.Disposition = DispositionRetry
210			}
211		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
212			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
213			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonNeedsReview
214		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition:
215			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
216			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonDecomposition
217		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed:
218			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
219			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonPushFailed
220		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict:
221			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
222			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonPushConflict
223		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman:
224			o.Disposition = DispositionNeedsHuman
225		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed:
226			// Detail carrying a valid ParkReason disambiguates Park from LoopError.
227			if pr := ParkReason(r.Detail); ParkReasonValid(pr) {
228				o.Disposition = DispositionPark
229				o.ParkReason = pr
230			} else {
231				o.Disposition = DispositionLoopError
232			}
233		}
234		return o
235	}

  <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.

424	func TestRefactorBaseline_FixturesStable(t *testing.T) {
425		dir := filepath.Join("testdata", "refactor_baseline")
426		update := os.Getenv("UPDATE_BASELINE") == "1"
427	
428		names := make([]string, 0, len(refactorBaselineScenarios))
429		for name := range refactorBaselineScenarios {
430			names = append(names, name)
431		}
432		sort.Strings(names)
433	
434		for _, name := range names {
435			events := refactorBaselineScenarios[name]
436			t.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {
437				var canonical bytes.Buffer
438				gotKinds := make([]string, 0, len(events))
439				for _, ev := range events {
440					if k, _ := ev["kind"].(string); k != "" {
441						gotKinds = append(gotKinds, k)
442					}
443					line, err := json.Marshal(ev)
444					if err != nil {
445						t.Fatalf("marshal: %v", err)
446					}
447					canonical.Write(line)
448					canonical.WriteByte('\n')
449				}
450	
451				want, ok := refactorBaselineExpectedKinds[name]
452				if !ok {
453					t.Fatalf("scenario %q missing expected-kinds entry", name)
454				}
455				if !equalStringSlices(gotKinds, want) {
456					t.Fatalf("scenario %q: kinds=%v want=%v", name, gotKinds, want)
457				}
458	
459				path := filepath.Join(dir, name)
460				if update {
461					if err := os.WriteFile(path, canonical.Bytes(), 0o644); err != nil {
462						t.Fatalf("write: %v", err)
463					}
464					return
465				}
466	
467				got, err := os.ReadFile(path)
468				if err != nil {
469					t.Fatalf("read fixture %s: %v (run UPDATE_BASELINE=1 to regenerate)", path, err)
470				}
471				if !bytes.Equal(got, canonical.Bytes()) {
472					t.Fatalf("fixture %s drifted from canonical baseline; run UPDATE_BASELINE=1 to regenerate after intentional event-shape changes", path)
473				}
474			})
475		}
476	
477		t.Run("no_orphan_fixtures", func(t *testing.T) {
478			entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
479			if err != nil {
480				t.Fatalf("read dir: %v", err)
481			}
482			for _, e := range entries {
483				if e.IsDir() || filepath.Ext(e.Name()) != ".jsonl" {
484					continue
485				}
486				if _, ok := refactorBaselineScenarios[e.Name()]; !ok {
487					t.Errorf("orphan baseline fixture %s has no scenario in refactorBaselineScenarios", e.Name())
488				}
489			}
490		})
491	}

493	func equalStringSlices(a, b []string) bool {
494		if len(a) != len(b) {
495			return false
496		}
497		for i := range a {
498			if a[i] != b[i] {
499				return false
500			}
501		}
502		return true
503	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
45		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
46		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
47		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
48		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
49		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
50		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
51	}
52	
53	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
54	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
55	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
56	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
57	
58	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
59	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
60	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
61	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
62	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
63	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
64	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
65	
66	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
67	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
68	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
69	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
70	// advance the base branch.
71	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
72	
73	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
74	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
75	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
76	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:

39	// PostMergeReviewOutput carries the review state machine's decision back to
40	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
41	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
42	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
43	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
44	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.

47		Approved   bool
48		StoreErrOp string
49		StoreErr   error
50	}
51	
52	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
53	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer, --no-review,
54	// or the review:skip label) the bead is closed and the function returns
55	// Approved=true. Otherwise the reviewer is invoked, its verdict (or error)
56	// drives the canonical event emissions (review / review-error /
57	// review-manual-required / review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict
58	// the bead is reopened and the triage policy is consulted via the private
59	// applyReviewTriageDecision callout that previously lived as a method on
60	// ExecuteBeadWorker.
61	//
62	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
63	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed BLOCK, or reviewer
64	// transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp + StoreErr so
65	// callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern unchanged.
66	//
67	// This is the C3 (ddx-a921ff01) extraction of the 180-line review block
68	// previously inline in execute_bead_loop.go; the loop now sees a structured
69	// outcome and conflict-recovery / decomposition / no_changes paths remain
70	// in the loop body until subsequent C-series beads (C4, C5) move them too.
71	func RunPostMergeReview(ctx context.Context, in PostMergeReviewInput) PostMergeReviewOutput {
72		report := in.Report
73		out := PostMergeReviewOutput{Report: report, Approved: true}
74	
75		now := in.Now
76		if now == nil {
77			now = time.Now
78		}
79	
80		reviewSkipped := in.Reviewer == nil || in.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip")
81	
82		if reviewSkipped {
83			if err := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
84				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
85				out.StoreErr = err
86				return out
87			}
88			out.Report = report
89			return out
